action: Open token selection and review for allowlisted assets including two sharing a symbol, and inspect name, symbol, logo or fallback, full address, decimals, network, and verification state.
actor: A user choosing a financial asset

expectedAssertions.statement: Every asset exposes complete identity and verification or risk state at the financial decision point.
Same-symbol assets remain distinguishable by full address and network, with a deterministic logo fallback.

negativePaths.trigger: Present same-symbol different-address assets and make one configured logo unavailable.

prerequisites: Fixtures include a missing-logo asset and same-symbol distinct-address assets.
recovery: Use full address and network to select the intended asset.
Return to selection if review identity differs.
title: Complete token identity

action: Search approved tokens by symbol, name, and address, then enter no-match, malformed, unsupported, missing-metadata, and token-list versus indexer disagreement inputs.
actor: A user searching for a token

expectedAssertions.statement: Supported searches return only matching approved assets with unambiguous identity.
Invalid or unsupported states are explained distinctly and cannot become executable arbitrary imports.

negativePaths.trigger: Enter a malformed or unapproved address, or return conflicting metadata for one address.

prerequisites: Approved, unsupported, malformed, missing-metadata, and disagreement fixtures are available.
recovery: Correct the query and choose an approved asset.
title: Approved token search

action: Attempt execution with a same-token pair, zero or no-code address, disallowed quote asset, unsupported decimals, out-of-policy asset, known fee-on-transfer or rebasing asset, and one approved standard asset.
actor: A user selecting assets for a write

expectedAssertions.statement: Invalid identity, pair, quote-policy, decimal, and capability combinations are rejected before simulation or signing.
Known incompatible assets are blocked without claiming generic detection, and unexpected simulation or balance-delta behavior cannot produce false success.

negativePaths.trigger: Select a prohibited asset or make standard-token simulation and balance deltas disagree.

prerequisites: Policy fixtures cover each prohibited class and an approved token.
Simulation and wallet call counts are observable.
recovery: Refresh balances and simulation before retrying.
title: Token policy preflight rejection

action: Enter and apply Max for 6, 8, and 18 decimal tokens across zero, dust, huge, exact-balance, overprecision, scientific-notation, overflow, and over-balance values.
actor: A user entering a token amount

expectedAssertions.statement: Valid inputs and Max convert to exact base units without rounding above balance, including zero, dust, and huge supported values.
Overprecision, overflow, scientific notation, and over-balance input block execution with a specific correction.

negativePaths.trigger: Enter unsupported precision, scientific notation, overflow, or one base unit above balance, then apply Max.

recovery: Refresh balance and apply Max again.
title: Decimal and Max boundaries

action: Apply Max to native ETH and an ERC-20 for equivalent writes, compare selected amounts with ETH balance and buffered gas, and repeat after gas conditions change.
actor: A user spending a maximum balance

expectedAssertions.statement: Native Max reserves buffered gas and never selects the full ETH balance when gas is required.
Token Max uses no more than token balance, checks ETH separately, and gas drift invalidates stale native Max.

negativePaths.trigger: Use native Max with no gas reserve, token Max with inadequate ETH, or increase gas after review.

prerequisites: Native and token balances plus gas estimates are controllable.
recovery: Refresh gas and recompute native Max.
Fund ETH separately for token execution.
title: Native and token Max semantics

action: Review an ERC-20 write with absent, sufficient, stale, and revoked allowance; inspect disclosure and exercise supported standard-bool and excluded approval-capability fixtures.

expectedAssertions.statement: Review shows exact token, amount or cap, full spender, current allowance, and persistence; sufficient allowance skips approval.
Absent, stale, or revoked allowance requires fresh preflight, and excluded approval behavior never reaches wallet submission under a generic claim.

negativePaths.trigger: Revoke allowance after review or select a token with excluded false-return, no-return, or zero-reset behavior.

prerequisites: Allowance states and approval capabilities are controllable.
recovery: Refresh allowance and create a new exact approval review.
Choose a token with supported behavior.
title: Exact approval capability policy

action: Prepare a withdrawal with LBPair operator approval absent, present, and externally revoked; inspect exact pair and router disclosure, then change pair or operator before final preflight.

expectedAssertions.statement: The interface distinguishes approveForAll from ERC-20 allowance and discloses full pair, operator, persistence across all bins, live state, and revocation instructions.
Matching approval skips the request; revoked, wrong-pair, wrong-operator, or stale approval blocks remove simulation pending fresh preflight.

negativePaths.trigger: Revoke approval after review or substitute another pair or operator while selected-bin disclosure is visible.

prerequisites: An owner has positive pair-bin balances.
Operator state and identity are controllable.
recovery: Review and grant approval for the exact pair and router.
title: Pair-wide operator disclosure

action: Review equivalent native ETH and ERC-20 WETH writes for the wrapped-native pool, inspecting identity, allowance requests, router function, and native value.
actor: A user choosing ETH or WETH semantics

expectedAssertions.statement: Native ETH never requests ERC-20 approval and uses the native router entry with exact native value.
WETH remains address-identifiable, follows ERC-20 approval rules, and cannot silently use native semantics.

negativePaths.trigger: Expose ETH approval or attempt WETH submission through a native-value path.

prerequisites: Native and token builders are available.
recovery: Refresh allowance or native balance for the selected semantics.
title: ETH and WETH separation

action: Open hookless, allowlisted, unknown, indexer-RPC mismatched, malformed, and runtime-code-changed pools; inspect hook identity and attempt swap, add, and remove preflight.
actor: A user entering a pool write

expectedAssertions.statement: Hookless or supported hooks expose exact identity and declared behavior before a write.
Unknown, malformed, mismatched, or changed hooks produce actionable read-only state with zero write simulation or wallet requests.

negativePaths.trigger: Return an unsupported or mismatched hook, or change runtime hook code after attestation.

prerequisites: Hook fixtures and observable call counts are available.
recovery: Restore a hookless or supported exact pool.
Refresh RPC attestation and obtain a new review.
title: Supported-hook fail-closed writes
